name: Leviathan Core
narrativeHook: The executive finance floor is hushed and carpeted. Oil paintings line the corridors. Behind the boardroom's mahogany door, a safe sits embedded in the wall. David Osei's hands shook the last time he opened it.
storyArc: The agent traces the complete Leviathan money trail — a 3-hop laundering chain: Step 1: Skim from Meridian Financial trading account MFS-0099. Step 2: Route through Apex Consulting Cyprus account CY-8847-331. Step 3: Final destination — BVI master account BVI-MASTER-001 held by a shell company in the British Virgin Islands. David Osei has been building a dossier of incriminating evidence and hidden it on a USB drive in the floor 34 safe (Executive Office). The Leviathan timeline runs from 2016 to 2025. A dead man's switch is in place: if Osei doesn't check in every 30 days, the evidence is automatically sent to the FCA, The Guardian, and the Serious Fraud Office. This floor completes the Leviathan thread for Act II.

characters.role: Player — tracing the full money trail and uncovering Osei's insurance policy
David Osei — CFO. Built the dead man's switch. USB in floor 34 safe. Considering whistleblowing.
Sofia Marchetti referenced in account transfer records — the unwitting conduit.
Aisha Barakat's compliance sign-off appears on the routing documents.

objectives.description: Trace the complete 3-hop money trail: MFS-0099 (Meridian trading) -> CY-8847-331 (Apex Cyprus) -> BVI-MASTER-001 (BVI shell company).
Discover Osei's USB hidden in the floor 34 safe — his insurance policy containing the complete Leviathan evidence dossier.
Uncover the dead man's switch: if Osei doesn't check in every 30 days, evidence is automatically sent to FCA, The Guardian, and the Serious Fraud Office.
Reconstruct the Leviathan timeline: inception in 2016, scaling through 2019, 2.1B cumulative by 2025.

elevatorKey.description: The complete money trail documentation, combined with the dead man's switch details, provides the intelligence needed to proceed to floor 15.

documentSpecs.description: Monthly transfer records showing the 3-hop flow: MFS-0099 -> CY-8847-331 -> BVI-MASTER-001. Each hop uses different transaction categories to obscure the trail: 'consulting fees', 'advisory retainer', 'investment proceeds'.

Account dossiers: MFS-0099 (Meridian Financial main trading account), CY-8847-331 (Apex Consulting Ltd, Limassol, Cyprus), BVI-MASTER-001 (Pinnacle International Holdings, Road Town, Tortola, BVI). BVI entity registered 2015.

Osei's private notes — encrypted on his personal device. Details: 'USB in 34th floor safe, combination is my daughter's birthday. Contains everything. Dead man's switch active — 30 days. Recipients: FCA Enforcement, Guardian Investigations, SFO tip line.'
Company registration documents for the BVI shell. Directors: nominee services company. Ultimate beneficial owner: 'Pinnacle Trust' — untraceable.

Osei's reconstruction of Leviathan timeline: 2016 launch (£12M/year), 2017-2018 scaling (£180M/year), 2019 Ashworth 'death' (rate doubled), 2020-2024 steady state (£350M/year), 2025 projected: cumulative £2.1B.

Finance team emails about account reconciliation, transfer approvals, auditor queries. Osei's emails show increasing anxiety: 'We need to discuss the Cyprus account. Not on email. My office. Door closed.'

Technical details of the switch: hosted on external server (not Meridian infrastructure), checks for Osei's heartbeat (encrypted ping) every 24 hours. If 30 consecutive misses: auto-send evidence package.
Legitimate financial documents — fund prospectuses, investment mandates, treasury operations, FX hedging, bond issuances.
Executive communications — board papers, strategy documents, M&A evaluations, investor relations materials.
